methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Citra Space Corporation is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Tom Nichols and Daniel Brunski. The company has raised $15M to date. The valuation landscape includes six public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 25.8x price-to-sales, alongside eight private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Given data constraints, only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M total raised as a proxy for post-money value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progression, yielding $30M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage or valuation-tier comparables within the narrow space sector (0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier peers—below the minimum threshold of 3). Public-comp anchoring using the 25.8x sector P/S median could not be applied because the target's revenue is not publicly available, as is standard for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $34M, with a median point of $30M. This range reflects conservative (low) and optimistic (high) iterations of the stage-progression method rather than true triangulation across independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, scarcity of narrow peer comparables at this stage, and whether the 2.0x step-up multiplier appropriately captures sector-specific risk and growth dynamics. Additional financials would strengthen conviction materially.
